Lines.ui.pause.title: Paused
Lines.ui.settings.title: Settings
Lines.ui.tutorial.1: Hey, kiddo! It's been a while. I can't come to you — everything's flooded and the world has gone grey.
Lines.ui.tutorial.2: Figure out what's going on around here.
Lines.ui.tutorial.3: Walk up to the car. E — get in or get out.
Lines.ui.tutorial.4: W — forward, Mouse — look around, A/D — tilt, Space — up, Ctrl — down.
Lines.ui.tutorial.5: Find Grandpa Miron or Lev and have a chat. Press E to talk.
Lines.ui.tutorial.6: Someone needs a color. Open the trunk (LMB) and toss in the crafting resources.
Lines.ui.tutorial.7: Grab the axe off the roof (hold LMB) and chop some wood — logs will drop.
Lines.ui.tutorial.8: Drag 3 logs (hold LMB) into the trunk.
Lines.ui.tutorial.9: Also grab 2 pieces of resin and toss them in the same way.
Lines.ui.tutorial.10: Now climb into the trunk yourself!
Lines.ui.tutorial.12: Press the egg to hatch a monster. The monster's color depends on the pen.
Lines.ui.tutorial.13: Grab the brush from storage and pet your monster until a <gradient="RGBW">pigment</gradient> drops.
Lines.ui.tutorial.14: Monsters have emotions and moods that affect the <gradient="RGBW">pigment</gradient>. Higher <color=green>health</color> means more pigment volume. Happiness affects saturation.
Lines.ui.tutorial.15: You need to mix a color that matches the photo in the book (TAB).
Lines.ui.tutorial.16: Raw <gradient="RGBW">pigment</gradient> needs to be ground into powder. Find the mortar and drop the pigment in.
Lines.ui.tutorial.17: Pick up the pestle (hold LMB), move it up and down to grind until it's done.
Lines.ui.tutorial.18: If you put multiple <gradient="RGBW">pigments</gradient> in the mortar, they'll blend together when ground!
Lines.ui.tutorial.19: Place the ground <gradient="RGBW">pigment</gradient> of the right color into the <color=blue>Infuser</color>.
Lines.ui.tutorial.20: Need a darker shade? Add some <color=black>resin</color> using the valve on the pipe to the right (LMB).
Lines.ui.tutorial.21: Toss some firewood into the furnace. The temperature gauge will start filling up.
Lines.ui.tutorial.22: Paint can only be brewed at <color=orange>maximum temperature</color>, so don't hold back on the wood!
Lines.ui.tutorial.23: Pull the lever (LMB) once the <color=orange>indicator fills the gauge — your paint is ready!</color>
Lines.ui.tutorial.24: Bring the finished paint to the client and have a chat.
Lines.ui.tutorial.25: Some items can be carried between worlds.
Lines.ui.tutorial.26: That's all the basics — the rest is up to you!
Lines.ui.tutorial.27: Grandpa has left the chat (T — close)
Lines.ui.validator.info:
An error occurred while reading the save file. You'll have to delete the game :(
Tip: don't touch the files if you don't know what they do.
Lines.ui.validator.title: !!!WARNING!!!
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.1: Monsters can get hungry, sad, happy, or scared.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.2: You can check their stats on the panel in the top left.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.3: All of this affects the <gradient="RGBW">pigment</gradient>. Check the book for the details.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.4: Your little monster is getting hungry. Feed it a <color=orange>carrot.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.5: Toss the seeds into the trunk, then onto the garden bed. Wait for them to sprout.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.6: Feeding boosts Happiness and reduces Hunger.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.7: If <color=blue>Discontent</color> is high, carry it outside the pen for a bit — it'll appreciate the change.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.8: The higher the <color=blue>Discontent</color>, the lower the <color=yellow>Happiness.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.9: If monsters of different colors are placed in neighboring pens, their <color=blue>Fear</color> will grow.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.10: If same-colored monsters are placed next to each other, their <color=green>Curiosity</color> will increase.
Lines.ui.additional_tutorial.11: Looks like the <gradient="RGBW">color</gradient> didn't quite match — try adding more or less <color=black>resin</color>, or mix different <gradient="RGBW">pigments.
Lines.book.cooking.infuser: Drop it in the infuser (add resin if needed)
Lines.book.cooking.lever: Toss in some firewood and pull the lever
Lines.npc.DedMiron.name: Grandpa Miron
Lines.npc.DedMiron.task: Long ago I heard that colors are tied to emotions. Bring me the color from the book in that photo. It was as rich and vibrant as the grass on the lawn where we used to read together. I think it might help.
Lines.npc.Lev.name: Lev
Lines.npc.Lev.task: Here, take a photo of that ball — yeah, I did break a window with it, but it was a gift from grandpa. Means a lot to me. It was as bright and warm as the sun on the days we played together.
Lines.npc.Nadeshda.name: Nadezhda
Lines.npc.Nadeshda.task: Here's a photo of the rug I gave him after he fixed my tap. Its color feels like warmth and care to me — and that's what I want to give Matvey as a thank you.
Lines.npc.Matvey.name: Matvey
Lines.npc.Matvey.task: Take this photo of the shovel — Nadezhda once said its color matched my resolve and strength. But that strength has faded. Bring it back, or we may never see each other again. I can't live with that shame.
Lines.npc.Aglaya.name: Aglaya
Lines.npc.Aglaya.task: Here — I was making her a butterfly for her birthday, but never got to give it. I made it as light as a cherry blossom petal. I want to deliver it in person, as a sign of making peace.
Lines.npc.Kassandra.name: Kassandra
Lines.npc.Kassandra.task: I've heard that power and stability are tied to the color of my inkwell. That color once stood for wisdom and authority, and the ink — for the unwavering certainty of my decisions. I lost it the moment I began to doubt myself. I need to find that color again, and with it — my confidence.
Lines.npc.Daria.name: Daria
Lines.npc.Daria.task: I remember when Aglaya asked me to make her a dress for graduation. What a project that was! My best work — had to be, for a friend like her. I ordered velvet as pure as an open sky. Here's a photo. The look on her face — I'll never forget it. I think this could help.
Lines.npc.Zahar.name: Zakhar
Lines.npc.Zahar.task: Here's a photo of the lock on my study — in there I can finally clear my head and let go of everything, so I can talk to her without losing it.
Lines.book.monsters.1: When your monsters get hungry, feed them <color=orange>carrots</color>. Hunger affects <color=green>Health</color>.
Lines.book.monsters.2: Take your monster outside its pen to lower its <color=blue>Discontent.
Lines.book.monsters.3: Monsters have their own weather preferences — keep an eye on them.
Lines.book.monsters.4: Monsters in neighboring pens can <color=blue>scare</color> each other, or boost each other's <color=green>Curiosity</color>. See for yourself =)
Lines.ui.hatch.title: I'll name my monster...
Lines.tutorial.output.talk: Hey kiddo! I know things got rough between you and your dad, and you disappeared. Coming back isn't easy — but first, figure out what's happening out there.
Lines.tutorial.output.openTrunk: Someone needs a color — time to brew one. You'll need resources first.<color=orange> Open the trunk by holding LMB and pulling up.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.jump: Now hop in yourself — just jump. No questions.
Lines.tutorial.output.smalWalk: So now you know the secret of this old clunker. Well done! Take a look around.
Lines.tutorial.output.eggPress: Find an egg in the <color=green>green pen</color> and <color=orange>press</color> it to hatch a monster.
Lines.tutorial.output.brushPick: Time to brew a color! <color=orange>Grab the brush from storage.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.pigmentDrop: Pet the monster until it drops a pigment. Its <color=green>size depends on Health</color>, and <color=green>saturation on Happiness.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.dusted: <color=orange>Press E and grind</color> it with the pestle.
Lines.tutorial.output.infuserDrop: Drop the ground pigment <color=orange>into the slot on the Infuser.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.logsBurn: Now fire it up — <color=orange>toss some logs into the furnace</color> from the storage.
Lines.tutorial.output.infuserPress: <color=orange>Press E near</color> the Infuser to <color=orange>start brewing!</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.valveSpin: If you need a slightly darker color, add some resin using the valve on the right.
Lines.tutorial.output.leave: Bring the finished paint to the client <color=orange>through the portal.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.axeOnCar: Help the others — toss the axe onto the car roof. Paint buckets can go up there too.
Lines.tutorial.output.carSit: Now <color=orange>get in the car with E.</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.ad: Try <color=orange>turning the car with A/D</color>, <color=orange>tilt — Mouse</color>
Lines.tutorial.output.last: That's everything — now <color=orange>fly over to Lev</color> and give him a hand!
